_strategy: R1: 1/10 +1 (ad/NetworkLoss), 5 -1, 4 HTTP env-fail (tproxy stale on R1-touched pods). R2 strategy: (1) avoid R1-touched services for HTTP; (2) double down on Network types (R1 winner); (3) pair (30%) on services we expect synergy; (4) fresh ns via --auto --allow-bootstrap so tproxy state is clean.
_results: 5 traces with terminal: 3/5 +1 (p04 accounting/NetworkLoss80→postgresql, p06+p07 paired recommendation/NetworkPartition→flagd × image-provider/PodKill → result.collection — both inherit, p10 email/NetworkDelay3s→checkout), 2/5 -1 (p05 cart/NetworkBandwidth→flagd, p08 quote/PodKill). 1 pair p01+p02 ad NetworkLoss/NetworkPartition→flagd: backend returned items=[] (ns/trace null in runs.jsonl) — guided resolver may have rejected; treat as 0. 2 env errors (500): p03 checkout/NetworkDelay→quote pair not in observed_pairs (otel-demo's checkout→quote gRPC not yet seen by tproxy at submit time), p09 fraud-detection/DNSError checkout (domain=checkout shorthand not resolved as service). Signal: Network>HTTP for otel-demo detector; PodKill ineffective except when paired with active network chaos.

candidates._failure: backend returned 200 with empty items array; submit_dual logged null trace/ns. Likely guided resolver dropped second spec on fork-related ns reuse

guided resolver: target pair (checkout, quote) not in observed network pairs at submit time. otel-demo loadgen takes ~30s to warm; pre-submit observation window may be too short, or quote service uses non-HTTP transport that tproxy does not surface

candidates._note: Pair winner: NetworkPartition flagd from recommendation + PodKill image-provider in same ns; both inherit +1

candidates._failure: guided resolver: DNSError domain=checkout did not resolve. Need fully-qualified domain (e.g. checkout.otel-demo.svc.cluster.local) or external host (e.g. example.com); short service name not accepted by chaos-mesh DNSChaos resolver

_known_issues.p03_network_pair_unobserved: When submitting NetworkDelay/Loss/etc. between two services via submit_dual.py direct API path, the guided resolver pre-checks observed network pairs from rcabench's traffic snapshot (Redis key network:pairs:<ns>). If pair not yet observed, request 500s. Workaround: warm loadgen for 30-60s before submit, OR use --skip-pair-check (if/when added — file issue).
_known_issues.p09_dns_short_name: DNSError domain= must be FQDN or external host. Short service name 'checkout' is not resolved by chaos-mesh DNSChaos (which uses CoreDNS-level interception, requires real DNS query). Workaround: use 'example.com' for DNS denial test, or 'checkout.otel-demo.svc.cluster.local'.
_known_issues.p01_p02_pair_null_trace: Pair landed group_id but items[] empty. Suspect guided resolver/multi-spec validation race when bootstrap allocator assigns ns. Re-run for a clean signal in R3.
